update_note: Updated May 20, 2026 to incorporate Google I/O 2026 Android XR smart glasses launch (today), Samsung Galaxy Glasses July 2026 details, and Apple testing confirmation from April 2026.

sources.title: Intelligent eyewear with Gemini is coming this fall — Google I/O 2026 official announcement
claims_supported: Google and Samsung officially launched Android XR Gemini-powered smart glasses at I/O 2026
Two tiers: audio glasses (Fall 2026 first) and display glasses
Partners include Warby Parker and Gentle Monster
Features: Gemini navigation, contextual awareness, calls/texts, Uber and app integration
Pairs with both Android and iOS

sources.title: AI Smart Glasses to Quadruple Revenue in 2026 as Apple and Samsung Prepare to Enter the Market
claims_supported: Market revenue ~$1.2B in 2025 rising to $5.6B in 2026 (AI-enabled segment)
Apple and Samsung confirmed market entry in 2026
Meta scaling production to 20 million units in 2026

sources.title: Ray-Ban Maker EssilorLuxottica More Than Tripled Meta AI Glasses Sales in 2025
claims_supported: 7 million units sold in 2025, tripling year-over-year
EssilorLuxottica total revenue jumped 11% to ~€28.5 billion
Production target raised to 20–30 million units

sources.title: Meta Sold Over 7 Million Smart Glasses Last Year, Effectively Tripling Sales in 2025 Alone
claims_supported: 7M units sold in 2025 vs 2M units combined across all of 2023–2024
EssilorLuxottica stock surged 5% on the announcement

sources.title: Samsung's Galaxy Glasses: Why the July 2026 Launch Could Redefine AI Wearables
claims_supported: Samsung Galaxy Glasses (codenamed 'Jinju') reveal at London Unpacked July 2026
Estimated price $379–$499
Built-in speakers, mics, cameras; voice command-first
Powered by Android XR + Qualcomm silicon with Gemini AI

sources.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
claims_supported: Apple actively testing multiple smart glasses designs for late-2026 or 2027 launch
Apple has secured majority of global micro-OLED production capacity for 2026–2027

sources.title: Global Smart Glasses Shipments Soared 110% YoY in H1 2025, With Meta Capturing Over 70% Share
claims_supported: Smart glasses shipments up 110% year-over-year in H1 2025
Meta captures 73–82% of smart glasses market by shipment share
Xiaomi second largest by shipment share in H1 2025

sources.title: AI Glasses Market Poised to Hit 10 Million Units in 2026 — Omdia Forecasts
claims_supported: 10 million AI glasses units forecast for 2026
Category crossing the chasm from early adopters to mainstream

sources.title: Smart Glasses Market Size, Share & Trends Analysis Report, 2033
claims_supported: Smart glasses market CAGR and growth projections to 2033
Market size and segment breakdown by application and geography

sources.title: Wearable AI Market Size And Share — Industry Report 2033
claims_supported: Wearable AI market growing at 27.83% CAGR from 2026 to 2033
Market size $43.64B in 2025, reaching $55.69B in 2026

sources.title: Harvard Students Demonstrate AI and Meta Glasses Can Identify Strangers
claims_supported: Harvard students demonstrated I-XRAY — facial recognition via Ray-Ban Meta glasses
Triggered global privacy alarm and EU regulatory scrutiny

sources.title: AI Smart Glasses Privacy Concerns Trigger EU Probes, December 2025
claims_supported: EU triggered formal probes in December 2025 under GDPR and AI Act
Privacy concerns following I-XRAY demo and Meta footage contractor reports

sources.title: Meta Sued Over AI Smart Glasses Privacy Concerns After Workers Reviewed Intimate Footage
claims_supported: U.S. class-action lawsuit filed March 2026 against Meta
Contractors reviewed intimate user footage captured by Ray-Ban Meta glasses
First major regulatory test for AI wearables privacy

sources.title: Meta Invests $3.5 Billion in EssilorLuxottica in AI Glasses Push
claims_supported: Meta invested $3.5B in EssilorLuxottica — 3% stake
Partnership covers co-development of future Ray-Ban AI glasses generations
Signal of long-term bet on glasses as the primary consumer AI hardware

sources.title: CES 2026: Meta Ray-Ban Display Teleprompter, Neural Handwriting, Garmin Partnership
claims_supported: Teleprompter display mode for Ray-Ban Display glasses
Neural handwriting via EMG band
Garmin fitness tracking partnership

sources.title: How AI Glasses Are Used In Enterprise and Healthcare
claims_supported: 40%+ of North American hospitals expected to integrate smart glasses for surgical guidance
29% reduction in procedure times when AI-augmented wearables deployed
Warehouse logistics accuracy improvements with AI glasses guidance
sources.note: 29% procedure time reduction is from clinical tests cited by CMI — treat as directionally accurate; independent clinical study verification recommended before publication

unverified_claims.claim: Search volume ~450,000/mo for combined 'smart glasses' + 'AI glasses' + 'AI wearables' terms
unverified_claims.note: Estimated from market momentum signals and Counterpoint/Omdia unit data; exact Google Trends/Keywords Everywhere data not obtained. [unverified]
Estimated based on CES 2026 → Google I/O 2026 pipeline acceleration and Samsung Galaxy Glasses confirmation. No direct search volume 3M data obtained. [unverified]

unverified_claims.claim: Enterprise deployment 15–30% productivity gains in field service
unverified_claims.note: Cited directionally from multiple sources; specific percentage varies by deployment type and company. [partially verified]

unverified_claims.claim: Apple has secured majority of global micro-OLED production capacity for 2026–2027
unverified_claims.note: Reported by TechCrunch and Apple Insider citing supply chain sources; not independently confirmed by Apple. [partially verified]
